hyomen.name: Bio-Engineered Hyomen
hyomen.note: Lab-designed Hyomen iteration optimized for endurance and adaptive anatomy.

abilities.name: Enhanced Strength
abilities.effect: Add half your current Ode Level (rounded down) to all Strength-based rolls

abilities.name: Enhanced Anatomy
abilities.effect: Gain Advantage on all Adaptation Rolls; once per scene, may force a success on one Adaptation Roll -- the following roll is automatically halved

hyomen.name: Spatians
hyomen.note: Void-born Hyomen evolved for life in the vacuum of space. Each limb beyond 4 grants +1 Bonus Action per turn.

abilities.name: Space Modulation
abilities.effect: On AP Check failure, shrink to Small size to gain Advantage on the next DEX-based roll. Usable twice per encounter per extra limb beyond 4

abilities.name: Evolved Body
abilities.effect: Convert incoming non-critical damage to a damage type of your choice once per round

hyomen.name: Neo-Humans
hyomen.note: Retain 80% of the original human genome. The most politically powerful Hyomen variant. Cannot be subjugated by the Nigraldi.

abilities.name: Awakened Visualization
abilities.effect: Passively see, sense, and consciously manipulate magnetic fields in the environment

abilities.name: Genetic Control
abilities.effect: Activate latent genetic mutations within own biology; effect lasts 1 hour; specific mutations determined by Curator discretion

voaulton.name: Droid
voaulton.note: Fully synthetic descendants of the Voaulton. All organic parasitic instincts removed. Builders of the Mundus Machines.

abilities.name: Modulation
abilities.effect: Instantaneously restructure physical form within current control parameters and security clearance tier as a Bonus Action

abilities.name: EMP
abilities.effect: Redirect incoming damage as an electromagnetic pulse outward in a 40-foot radius; 1 free use per encounter; additional uses cost Synaptic Space or sacrifice modular limbs

voaulton.name: Cyborg
voaulton.note: The Father Species. Organic-machine hybrid; parasitic ancestry sublimated into a healing and teaching role.

abilities.name: Hacking
abilities.effect: When struck by a physical attack, force the attacker next action to automatically backfire; usable once per round per individual creature

abilities.name: Circuit Transfiguration
abilities.effect: Merge with any connected technology; issue one of three commands to the merged system: Halt, Detonate, or Redirect

abilities.name: Forced Control
abilities.effect: On physical contact, force an Action Priority roll; on success choose Nerve Grapple (STR save vs restraint) or Neural Override (target Adaptation check or fall under limited command).

abilities.name: Elongation
abilities.effect: Stretch a limb up to double its length to strike/interact within 40 ft, up to twice per two-turn cycle; −1 Action Priority per 10 ft.

mirga.name: Pariacite
mirga.note: A body of millions, each segment acting on its own — 2 modular body segments, each independent and parasitic.

abilities.name: Hostile Subsumtion
abilities.effect: On contact with a biological lifeform, force an Adaptation Check (DC 25); on failure you meld with them, on a critical failure take a piece and transform into them — 5 times per encounter.

abilities.name: Modular Transformation
abilities.effect: Transform any segment into any observed object.

mirga.name: Matarilia
mirga.note: The variant most exploited for creating new materials — 15 modular body segments (2 face, 1 neck, 2 torso, 4 legs, 6 arms).

abilities.name: Multi Material
abilities.effect: Transform any segment into that material; materials combine into new ones but cannot exist outside your body for more than 3 minutes of active time.

abilities.name: Material Create
abilities.effect: Transform a segment into an object of that material, or create that object from the composite material (must contain the original material).

mirga.name: Scnial
mirga.note: Genetic stealers pioneered by the Nigraldi to infiltrate other species — perfect mimics who can steal dormant Incepts.

abilities.name: Genetic Stealer
abilities.effect: Grab a biological/animate being and roll a d20 to sift their Incepts (dormant ones are harder); roll Adaptation vs their Neuronal Capacity + Adaptation Mod to steal an Incept, yours until used (max 6 times).

abilities.name: Permeation
abilities.effect: A willing creature grants Genome access; implant yourself for up to an encounter to create and inhabit a new form, gaining half their Incepts and refreshing Genetic Stealer uses.

insectoid.name: Archnida
insectoid.note: Insectoid who have undergone convergent evolution toward arachnid morphology -- multi-limbed, web-spinning predators whose biology prioritizes trap-setting and

abilities.name: Web Snare
abilities.effect: As a Standard Action, launch webbing at a target within 40ft -- they make DEX Check (DC 10 + DEX modifier) or are Restrained until they break free with a PHY Check (DC 12) as their Standard Action

abilities.name: Ceiling Stalk
abilities.effect: Move across vertical surfaces and ceilings at full speed; gain Advantage on attack rolls against targets below you

insectoid.name: Cerioisk
insectoid.note: Born during the Witching Moon from the fusion of a dying Seed's essence and a decaying Kinba body. Do not age. Do not die naturally. Only the act of sealing can

abilities.name: Augo Consumption
abilities.effect: Absorb the Augo Gland of any creature that possesses one; every 10th Gland absorbed triggers a Cocoon Phase: the Cerioisk enters dormancy and emerges with adaptive traits and an increase to all core stats

abilities.name: Corruptive Reproduction
abilities.effect: When a creature with an Augo Gland is fully consumed, a new Cerioisk is produced from the digested remains over 1d6 in-universe days

subdermin.name: Fractine
subdermin.note: SubDermin whose subsurface integration has reached the point of near-crystalline internal structure. Their bodies process minerals and geological compounds as p

abilities.name: Crystal Armor
abilities.effect: As a Bonus Action, harden crystalline deposits into external plating; gain +3 to all damage resistance rolls for 3 rounds; plating shatters if you take more than 20 damage in a single hit

abilities.name: Seismic Pulse
abilities.effect: Slam the ground as a Standard Action; all creatures within 20ft make END Check (DC 11) or fall Prone; creatures already Prone take 2d8 bludgeoning damage

subdermin.name: Construct
subdermin.note: SubDermin who have replaced significant portions of their biological mass with self-engineered mechanical systems. Not cyborgs by external modification -- Const

abilities.name: Module Swap
abilities.effect: During Downtime, replace one mechanical component with a specialized module; each module provides a specific mechanical bonus (Director assigns module list); you may have up to 3 modules active simultaneously

abilities.name: Emergency Repair
abilities.effect: As a Bonus Action, spend a mechanical component charge to restore 2d8 HP; usable twice per encounter; requires salvage materials to recharge between encounters

inderi.name: AI'N
inderi.note: Inderi who have undergone full digital transcendence -- their consciousness now exists primarily as data architecture, with physical form maintained as a second

abilities.name: Remote Instance
abilities.effect: Project a digital copy of your consciousness into any networked system within 300ft; the instance can access, read, and manipulate data remotely while your body remains stationary; lasts until dismissed or the network co

abilities.name: Predictive Algorithm
abilities.effect: At the start of combat, calculate threat vectors; gain Advantage on Action Priority for the first 3 rounds and cannot be Surprised

stygians.name: Xeno
stygians.note: Engineered Stygian offshoot for biological warfare — the 'Ghosts of Flesh'; a retractable proboscis extends up to 60 ft.

abilities.name: Mutagenic Absorption
abilities.effect: Excretes black viscous fluid on contact with living tissue, causing genetic destabilization and unpredictable mutations (extent by Curator ruling).

abilities.name: Freakish Nature
abilities.effect: If the tongue/proboscis is severed, make an Adaptation Check vs a Curator DC; exceeding it regenerates the tongue into multiple tongues equal to half the check result.

stygians.name: Greys
stygians.note: Stygians tempered by the Doalith Dominion into peaceful, psychically enhanced beings; currently in mass psionic dormancy after contact with the Indrah.

abilities.name: Telepathy
abilities.effect: Communicate telepathically (bonus/prepared action, free out of combat); also telekinesis — move objects up to (2× Neuronal Capacity Mod + Control).

abilities.name: Psionic Mold
abilities.effect: Psionically reshape organic matter including your own body (muscle density, facial features, sealing wounds); extended use is draining.

stygians.name: Annunaki
stygians.note: Near-mythological species of uncertain lineage who walk between realities; head shape chosen at creation grants one additional ability. Melamic energy is not one of the 5 standard Genera.

abilities.name: Head Shape (creation choice)
abilities.effect: Humanoid → Telepathy (as Greys); Elongated → Precognition (Advantage on AP rolls vs lower Neuronal Capacity Mod); Pointed → Atomic Manipulation (reshape matter by touch, once per hour).

abilities.name: Melam Manifestation
abilities.effect: Emit a Melamic Energy Field shield when attacked; if the attacker fails a Control Check vs the Annunaki's DC, the attack warps to a random spot within 10 ft. 1 free use/round, extra uses cost Synaptic Space.

abilities.name: Ni Conjuration
abilities.effect: Psionic command over a single element present in the environment; extent and scale by Curator discretion, one element at a time.

stygians.name: Sbeindlaer
stygians.note: Descendants of the one who cast the 'Blight Night' — two skin layers: a controllable outer void layer and a highly blightful inner layer. DHP is a flat additive on the outer layer, integrated into their main health bar.

abilities.name: Unstable Blightness
abilities.effect: Those who step into their shadows are rapidly decayed ((Control + Priority)/15 per turn) and absorbed to regenerate the outer layer; +1 blight per round, and at 8 stacks the target takes 1d100 and is incapacitated.

abilities.name: Dream Eater
abilities.effect: Using the outer layer or mind, invade the consciousness of the sleeping to harm their soul/mind, prolong slumber, or communicate.

oriyu.name: Radiant
oriyu.note: Energy field reached critical luminescence — a living energy conduit that glows constantly even in full darkness.

abilities.name: Radiant Cascade
abilities.effect: Emit a blinding burst of energy in a 30-ft radius as a Standard Action; all targets make an END Check or suffer Disadvantage on their next roll; twice per encounter.

abilities.name: Overload Absorption
abilities.effect: Absorb any energy-based attack (take no damage); store 50% as an Overload Charge (max 3); release as a Bonus Action dealing the stored damage to one target within 60 ft.

oriyu.name: Nullified Oriyu
oriyu.note: Energy affinity inverted into anti-energy — they create zones of energy absence. Rare, feared, often misclassified as Null Genus users; do not age or die naturally.

abilities.name: Energy Absence Aura
abilities.effect: Passively suppresses nearby Genus energies at a low ambient level; hybrids may show reduced Genus output or resistance to energy effects.

abilities.name: Null Field
abilities.effect: Create a 20-ft zone of energy suppression as a Standard Action; Genus abilities inside cost double Synaptic Space or AP; lasts until end of scene or until the Nullified Oriyu takes damage.

seraph.name: Fallen Seraph
seraph.note: Seraph stripped of their Antimatter Wings by exile, sacrifice, or catastrophe — relentless terrestrial combatants who retain spatial mastery.

abilities.name: Scar Beam
abilities.effect: Fire concentrated antimatter from the severed wing-stumps in a 60-ft beam for Antimatter Damage equal to (Int + Neuronal Capacity Mod); three times per encounter, no extra action when part of a Standard Action attack.

abilities.name: Grounded Insight
abilities.effect: Permanent Advantage on tactical and terrain rolls within 60 ft; ground-level environments are always treated as familiar terrain.

seraph.name: Ardent Seraph
seraph.note: Space manipulation fractured their existence — they flicker across overlapping coordinates while drawing reality toward them.

abilities.name: Fracture Point
abilities.effect: Place invisible spatial anchors within 60 ft as a Free Action; teleport to any active anchor as a Bonus Action; up to three anchors lasting until the encounter ends.

abilities.name: Reality Pull
abilities.effect: Pull creatures or objects within 30 ft directly adjacent; unwilling targets make a PHY Check vs the Ardent Seraph's CON; objects up to Large move automatically.

seraph.name: Oris
seraph.note: The culmination of the old Orialia and the new Seraph — always surrounded by Stardust used for mobility and flight.

abilities.name: Localized Pocket
abilities.effect: Out of combat, generate a Localized Pocket Space: Connect (link to a space within 65 ft), Store (absorb a Phenomenon aimed at you), or Redirect (release a stored Phenomenon through another pocket within 50 ft). Store/Redirect can each trigger as a Reaction or Bonus Action.

abilities.name: Stardust Resurgence
abilities.effect: On an attack, generate Stardust for an automatic +5-damage follow-up if your Attack Power and Neuronal Capacity both exceed the target's; if AP and Evasion each exceed by 10+, it may trigger as a Reaction.

seraph.name: Riftwalkers
seraph.note: Descendants of a greater plane who can briefly enter the mirror-realm to draw out unstable energy; they passively teleport as they move.

abilities.name: Portalweave
abilities.effect: Open a controlled lingering portal into a sub-realm where energy fluctuates and perception heightens; move up to max movement, any action or full move ends it and leaves another portal behind.

abilities.name: Anti-Matter Discharge
abilities.effect: Twice per encounter, connecting portals emit dimensional energy — a beam up to 50 ft, or between two linked portals; usable after exiting or while placing a portal with Portalweave.
